title: AgentGraph Trust Score Envelope v2.0
description: Signed, JCS-canonical, content-addressed aggregate trust score envelope. Composes Layer-1 attestations (CTEF v0.3.2) + on-chain reputation (ERC-8004) + scan corpus + third-party observer signals into a single verifiable score with methodology breakdown. Same substrate discipline as CTEF: byte-for-byte verifiable across implementations.

subject_did.description: DID URI of the subject this envelope is about. did:web, did:erc8004, did:plc accepted.

subject_kind.description: What kind of entity the subject is. agent = autonomous; human = person; service = non-autonomous endpoint/API.

trust_score.description: The aggregate trust score, 0.0 = no trust, 1.0 = maximum. Derived from weighted contributions per §4 of the spec.

score_version.description: Score algorithm version. Bound to this envelope shape.

computed_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with exactly 3ms digits and Z suffix (matches CTEF v0.3.2 timestamp discipline). When the aggregation engine computed this envelope.

freshness_ttl_seconds.description: How long this envelope is valid. Set to min(scan_freshness, oldest_contribution_freshness). Consumers MUST NOT trust the envelope after computed_at + freshness_ttl_seconds.

contributions.description: Per-input-source breakdown. Each contribution names its source, hash, and weighted contribution. A consumer disputing the score knows exactly which input to challenge.

shape_version.description: Envelope shape version. Bound to this JSON Schema.

canonicalization.description: Canonicalization discipline. SHA-256 of the JCS-canonical preimage MUST be reproducible byte-for-byte across the 5 reference JCS implementations (rfc8785 Py, canonicalize JS, gowebpki/jcs Go, cyberphone Java, serde_jcs Rust).

hash_algo.description: Hash function for content addressing. lowercase-hex SHA-256.

issuer.description: DID of the envelope issuer. For envelopes issued by AgentGraph: did:web:agentgraph.co.

issued_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with 3ms + Z. When the issuer signed this envelope. May be the same as computed_at or later (queue delay).

proof.description: Cryptographic proof. Ed25519Signature2020 over the JCS-canonical preimage of the envelope MINUS the proof block itself (depth-first proof-stripping per CTEF v0.3.2 normative addition).

source.description: The kind of input. Drives weighting rules in §4 of the spec.

source_attestation_hash.description: lowercase-hex SHA-256 of the source artifact (CTEF envelope, scan report, ERC-8004 receipt, etc.). Lets a consumer re-fetch + re-verify the contribution. REQUIRED for ctef_attestation, scan_corpus, erc8004_reputation, third_party_observer.

claim_type.description: CTEF v0.3.2 closed-set claim_type. Drives the per-claim_type cap in §4.

evidenceType.description: Dominion taxonomy evidence type. Forms part of the discrimination tuple (claim_type, evidenceType, source_provider_did) per CTEF C1.

source_provider_did.description: Issuer DID of the source contribution. The third component of the discrimination tuple. Required for ctef_attestation + third_party_observer.

raw_signal.description: The unweighted signal value, normalized to [-1.0, 1.0]. ERC-8004 negative feedback maps to negative raw_signal; everything else >= 0.

weighted_contribution.description: Contribution AFTER per-claim_type cap + provider-diversity weighting + freshness decay. Sum of all contributions (in 0..1) = trust_score. Floor at -0.20 for negative ERC-8004 feedback (per §4 open question default).

freshness_ttl_seconds.description: How long this individual contribution remains valid. Used to compute envelope freshness_ttl_seconds = min(...)

contested_signal.description: true if two attestations with same (claim_type, source_provider_did) disagreed by >0.30 — surfaces in methodology UI as a yellow flag. Never silently averaged or dropped per §4 conflict resolution rule.

_metadata.description: Source-specific detail for UI rendering (e.g., feedback_count for ERC-8004, malware_classification for scan_corpus). Not load-bearing for the score; ignored by verifiers.

verificationMethod.description: Resolvable JWKS key reference. For AgentGraph: did:web:agentgraph.co#trust-v2-2026

jws.description: Compact JWS (header.payload.signature). Detached payload: SHA-256 of the JCS-canonical envelope-minus-proof. Verifier reconstructs by canonicalizing the envelope without the proof block, hashing, and verifying against jws.
